question: What governs threshold adaptation in retinal ganglion cell encoding?

theories.name: Adaptive Threshold Spike Response Theory
theories.summary: Retinal ganglion cell firing is governed by a spike response model whose threshold adapts to recent input statistics on two timescales.

statements.text: The post-spike threshold relaxes exponentially toward baseline with a fixed fast time constant.

supporting_papers.title: Spike response models of parasol cells

statements.text: A second slow threshold component scales with the variance of the stimulus over the preceding second.

theories.name: Population Decorrelation Theory
theories.summary: Threshold adaptation in individual cells implements a population-level decorrelation of the ganglion cell code.

statements.text: Adapted thresholds reduce pairwise spike-count correlations between neighboring ganglion cells.
Decorrelation strength predicted by the theory exceeds what is measured in mid-peripheral retina.

contradicting_papers.title: Strong decorrelation observed under photopic conditions

narrative: Two competing theories of RGC threshold adaptation, with supporting and contradicting literature per law.
